purpose: The binding v0.9.0-defining NAIVE-AGENT GATE: run the public gpuwrf-canary-d02-sample case through the GPU port CLI exactly as a clean-room agent would (the documented gate command) and dimension/finite-compare the generated wrfout against the bundled CPU-WRF reference. Run by the v0.9.0 RELEASE WORKER on the consolidated release-candidate branch.
honesty_policy: Report the strict gate verdict as the proof computes it. Do not relabel a FAIL as a PASS. Characterize the root cause precisely (correctness vs scope).

environment.gpuwrf_package: PYTHONPATH=src on the consolidated RC (qke->fp64 + validation-burst + readme + naive-gate assets folded)
environment.gpu_resource_lock: claimed for this run, released after

gate_command_as_run: PYTHONPATH=src JAX_PLATFORM_NAME=cuda taskset -c 0-3 python3 -m gpuwrf.cli run --namelist <DIR>/namelist.input --input-dir <DIR> --output-dir /tmp/v090_naive_gate/runs/canary_d02_sample --domain d02 --hours 1 --compare-cpu-dir <DIR>
what_the_gate_compared: Ran ONE forecast hour (init 2026-04-29_18:00:00 -> 2026-04-29_19:00:00 UTC) through the JAX GPU port over the public sample case, then (1) finiteness-checked every written field and (2) dimension-compared the generated wrfout_d02_2026-04-29_19:00:00 against the bundled CPU-WRF reference of the same basename (every NetCDF dimension name + length must match exactly).

results_breakdown.namelist_fail_closed_validation: PASS (sample namelist accepted; all schemes supported; pre-JAX)
results_breakdown.cli_accepted_sample: PASS (reached and executed the heavy GPU pipeline)
results_breakdown.forecast_ran: PASS (1 forecast hour advanced on GPU; wall ~487 s incl. one-time ~4.5 min XLA compile)
results_breakdown.all_finite_check: PASS (all_finite=true across 56 written prognostic+diagnostic fields; T2 276.3-293.7 K, U10 -4.3..+7.4, V10 -4.4..+7.1 m/s, PSFC 702.9-1011.0 hPa, Q2 0.003-0.011 kg/kg -- physically plausible 1h forecast)
results_breakdown.core_spatial_dims_match: PASS -- Time, DateStrLen, west_east(159), west_east_stag(160), south_north(66), south_north_stag(67), bottom_top(44), bottom_top_stag(45), soil_layers_stag(4) ALL match the CPU reference exactly
results_breakdown.station_score: NOT_RUN (joined_rows=0; the public sample bundles no AEMET obs, so the obs-scoring sub-step did not run -> this is WHY the pipeline verdict is PARTIAL not GREEN, not a forecast failure)

missing_dimensions_in_generated.category: stochastic-perturbation (SPPT/SKEBS/SPP) seed arrays -- a feature v0.9.0 does NOT implement (out of scope / fail-closed)
Noah-MP multi-layer snow internal state -- a land diagnostic sub-state the GPU writer does not emit
Noah-MP snow+soil layer geometry -- same land-diagnostic sub-state, not emitted

dimension_mismatch_detail.root_cause: The GPU port writes a focused 64-variable wrfout (core prognostic + key surface/diagnostic fields) rather than the full 375-variable CPU-WRF operational catalog. The 3 mismatched dimensions exist in the CPU reference ONLY to dimension variables the GPU writer does not emit -- stochastic-perturbation seed arrays (not a v0.9.0 feature) and Noah-MP internal multi-layer snow state (a land diagnostic). NONE of the meteorological/spatial/vertical/soil dimensions mismatch; the forecast is fully finite and physically plausible. This is a SCOPE difference (writer variable coverage), NOT a grid error, a transcription bug, or an instability.

honest_verdict: FAIL on the STRICT every-dimension-equality criterion as the proof computes it. The failure is NOT a correctness/finiteness/grid failure: the 1h GPU forecast ran finite and physically plausible with every core spatial/vertical/soil dimension matching the CPU-WRF reference exactly. The 3 dimension mismatches are non-meteorological land/stochastic sub-dimensions that exist only because the GPU writer emits a focused 64-variable subset and omits out-of-scope variables (SPPT/SKEBS/SPP seed arrays the port does not implement, and Noah-MP internal snow-layer diagnostics). The pipeline verdict is PARTIAL (not GREEN) because the sample bundles no AEMET observations so the obs-scoring sub-step was skipped (NOT_RUN), not because the forecast failed.
decision_for_manager_and_critic: The strict gate as currently defined (exact equality of ALL NetCDF dimensions) does not PASS on this RC. Two honest options for the manager + cross-model critic to arbitrate BEFORE the tag: (A) tighten the gate's PASS criterion to the meteorological/spatial+finiteness contract (core dims + all_finite), which this RC PASSES cleanly, and document the GPU writer's 64-var focused-subset scope explicitly (the seed/snow-layer dims are out-of-scope writer coverage, already consistent with the README's fail-closed stochastic-perturbation and land-diagnostic boundaries); OR (B) extend the GPU wrfout writer to emit the Noah-MP snow-layer diagnostic variables (TSNO/SNICE/SNLIQ/ZSNSO) so those dims appear, and keep the stochastic-seed dims as an explicitly-documented out-of-scope omission. Either way this is a writer-coverage / gate-definition question, NOT a forecast-correctness blocker. RELEASE WORKER did NOT relabel the FAIL as a PASS and did NOT tag.
